key_insight: Neural operators can directly infer physically admissible constitutive laws from full-field displacement and force data, replacing slow inverse optimization with a single learned forward pass.
engineering_relevance: This enables near-instant material characterization from experiments, which could accelerate calibration, model discovery, and digital twin workflows in solid mechanics.
method: Physics-Augmented Neural Operators (PANO) and Constitutive Artificial Neural Operators (CANO).
Encode displacement fields with Laplacian eigenfunctions for discretization-independent, noise-robust input representations.
Map infinite-dimensional measurements to hyperelastic strain-energy density functions constrained by physics.
evidence_signal: The models were trained on simulated displacement-force tuples across different material models and evaluated on unseen, noisy, missing-data, varying-discretization, and different-geometry cases.
watch_item: Performance depends on training with representative simulated constitutive behavior, so extrapolation to highly novel materials or experimental conditions may still be limited.
paper_title: Neural operators solve inverse problems for constitutive model discovery
